title: Stop Building "Agent Soup." Start Building Compilers.
excerpt: Founders burn millions on AI engineers and advanced LLMs. The symptom is feature drift. The cause isn't technical - it's a massive game of telephone.

title: A2A Negotiation Experiment: The Outreach That Worked
excerpt: I built a 25-agent closed-loop email network where autonomous agents negotiate without human intervention. Here's how I refined the outreach.

title: The Automation Pattern That Kept Repeating Across 6 Industries
excerpt: I spent a day running a structured automation discovery engine across six completely different industries. The exact same automation gap showed up in every single domain.

title: I Asked an AI to Find Automation Opportunities. It Found 42 in One Day.
excerpt: 42 concrete, scored, production-ready automation opportunities my AI found in a single day across six unrelated industries.

title: I Gave My AI a 250-Item Target. It Failed. Then It Fixed Itself.
excerpt: You know that feeling when you deploy an agent, walk away, come back, and something's wrong? That was us. Except our target was 250.

title: Issue Management as a System Property
excerpt: I believed good monitoring was enough. Dashboards. Alerts. Runbooks. And we fixed the same problems three times. Here's what I built instead.

title: Three Layers Is Not Enough - You Need a Feedback Loop That Closes
excerpt: The difference between a system that learns from failure and one that merely records it is a single architectural decision.

title: Your Agent Spends 30 Seconds Finding Files - Here's How I Cut It to 3ms
excerpt: Every time your agent needs to find a file, it scans the entire directory tree. Here's the architecture for a solution that reads the entire workspace in 3ms.

title: I Spent 3 Months Building My Agent. Here Are the 5 Failures That Almost Killed It.
excerpt: I went all-in on building a personal AI agent. I spent 3 months, went through 5 architectures, killed 2 email accounts. Here's exactly what failed.

title: The 5 Infrastructure Layers That Broke My Agent
excerpt: Everyone talks about building agents. No one talks about what breaks them. Here's the structural post-mortem of 5 separate infrastructure layers that failed.

title: The Agent Reasoning Fallacy
excerpt: Everyone is obsessed with agent reasoning. But a brilliant, isolated agent is just a glorified chatbot. The real bottleneck isn't reasoning. It's discovery.

title: The Agent Black Box Problem
excerpt: If you're building with agents, you have a massive trust problem. Agent skills have become opaque black boxes. Here's how to fix it with a skill ledger.

title: Stop Buying Startup Data. Start Owning It.
excerpt: Everyone says the future of startup intelligence is better SaaS. The truth: the data in those platforms is backward-looking. What you need is a self-hosted entity graph.

title: Stop Waiting for Lightning: Build an Ideation Engine
excerpt: I spent years waiting for the big idea. Then I realized I was treating ideation like a creative act. I should have been treating it like an engineering problem.

title: Intelligence Is Trivial. Reliability Is the New Frontier.
excerpt: I spent 6 months building our first agentic workflow the wrong way. We focused exclusively on intelligence. Then we went to production and everything broke.

title: Compliance-as-Code: The Bottleneck Nobody's Automating
excerpt: Every two weeks, the engineering team releases a new model iteration. And every two weeks, compliance has to manually update conformity assessments. That's broken.

title: Stop Obsessing Over Alignment. Start Building Auditability.
excerpt: You cannot trust what you cannot verify. Enterprise AI adoption isn't stalling because of alignment. It's stalling because of auditability.

title: Get Off the Crunchbase Treadmill
excerpt: Everyone thinks Crunchbase is the gold standard for startup intelligence. By the time it hits Crunchbase, the outreach window is closed. Build a real intelligence layer.

title: EchoWeave: Your Phone Is a Graveyard. Let's Make It a Storyteller.
excerpt: Your phone knows everything about you. But all that data is siloed and dead. Enter EchoWeave: an on-device personal narrative OS.

title: I Automated 90% of Shipping. Here's What I Learned.
excerpt: I used to think my value as a founder was shipping. I built an autonomous pipeline that takes an idea from chat to code without me touching the keyboard.

title: I Watched a 20-Year Negotiation Veteran Collapse. Not Because of AI. Because of Me + AI.
excerpt: I sat across from someone who's been negotiating since before I started my career. I didn't out-negotiate them. I out-prepared them with an LLM coach.

title: Every Token Is Rent: Building an Agent That Doesn't Forget
excerpt: I watched my agent re-read the same project files for the third time that morning. Every session, a complete hard reset. Here's the memory architecture that fixed it.

title: The Most Boring Automation I've Ever Built - And Why It's the Only One That Works
excerpt: It doesn't generate poetry. It doesn't write code. It just never stops finding ways to be more useful.
